topic: SEC-part6 — EXP-495 (Sydney–Perth consolidation) + EXP-548 (live-fetch paragraph correction) verification (q440)
change_summary: Decider commit c905aa1 applied EXP-495 patch 3 (final TLDR fix: '460 km' → 'inconsistent distances' in §7.1 TLDR). EXP-495 patches 1+2 (§6.10/§7.1 body Sydney-Perth consolidation, dropping the 3,893/4,352 numbers from the headline sentences) were applied in a prior decider run; this verification confirms all 3 plus EXP-548 (live-fetch paragraph correction in §2b.1 — WIN-004 SAA dropped, WIN-006/007 retained as the only live-data WINs) landed cleanly. Both EXP-495 and EXP-548 were marked integrated and archived from expansion-tracker.json in c905aa1.
our_argument_summary: SEC-part6 (Section 6: 'The Confirmed Predictions') is the long-form WIN-by-WIN exposition. The patches in scope here tighten three Sydney-Perth touchpoints (§6.10, §7.1 body, §7.1 TLDR) and one live-fetch enumeration (§2b.1). None of the patches change the structural argument; they consolidate redundant arithmetic into a single canonical §2.9 cross-reference and correct an outdated WIN-004 inclusion in the live-fetch list.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: EXP-548 reflects a prior finding that WIN-004 (SAA separation) is hardcoded, not live-fetched — monitor.py contains a static expected/observed pair. Removing WIN-004 from the live-fetch enumeration brings the prose into alignment with the corrected code_analysis tag.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: live-fetch enumeration now correctly limited to WIN-006 and WIN-007 (NOAA NP.xy magnetic pole drift). All other previously-claimed live-fetches were shown by prior curmudgeon reviews to be hardcoded.
data_sources_used: NOAA NP.xy (the only confirmed live source per code_analysis)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: N/A (paragraph correction, not an analytical claim).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: SEC-part6 is the multi-WIN exposition section; not tied to a single monitor.py WIN. The §2b.1 live-fetch enumeration claim is structurally accurate now that WIN-004 is dropped.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Sydney-Perth and live-fetch paragraphs are exposition, not relabeling claims.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: N/A — these are exposition paragraphs about the dome's own claims and code, not predictions of our own.

holes_found.description: EXP-495 patch 1 (part7 §7.1 body) was applied with a different replace pattern than the EXP-495 file specifies. The current part7 text reads 'The dome\'s own coordinate scaffold — built by MDS on road distances — disagrees with the Finsler formula on Sydney-Perth within the dome\'s own model (the 3,893 vs 4,352 km arithmetic is documented in §2.9 below).' — while the EXP-495 file's patch 1 replace_pattern would have removed the 3,893 / 4,352 numbers entirely from the §7.1 body and replaced them with a §2.9 cross-reference link (no numbers in the §7.1 body). Both versions move the consolidation in the right direction; the actually-landed version retains the 3,893 / 4,352 numbers inside a parenthetical pointer to §2.9, while the EXP-authored version dropped them entirely. The EXP-495 file rationale for patch 1 explicitly says 'Drops 3,893 / 4,352 / 460 numbers from §7.1 body.' The 460 number IS gone (verified — grep -c '460 km' part7 returns 0). The 3,893 and 4,352 numbers REMAIN in §7.1 body inside the parenthetical 'the 3,893 vs 4,352 km arithmetic is documented in §2.9 below.' Effect: §7.1's body still has the inconsistent numbers, just bracketed and cross-referenced.
holes_found.recommendation: Accept as-is OR re-apply the verbatim EXP-495 patch 1 replace_pattern. The current state is a soft compromise: §7.1 body still carries 3,893/4,352 but flags them as cross-ref material. The EXP-authored version would have been cleaner (numbers live only in §2.9). Severity is minor because the section's structural argument is unaffected; the consolidation rationale is partially met (460 dropped) and partially unmet (3,893/4,352 retained). If the decider wants exact-EXP fidelity, re-apply patch 1's full replace verbatim. If the decider prefers the current parenthetical-with-numbers version, mark EXP-495 patch 1 as 'applied with soft modification' in applied-patches/ for audit-trail clarity.

stronger_arguments.description: The current §7.1 phrasing 'the 3,893 vs 4,352 km arithmetic is documented in §2.9 below' is arguably MORE useful to the reader than the verbatim EXP-authored replacement, because it primes the reader for the §2.9 arithmetic before they navigate there. The dome-defender attack on this paragraph (which the original EXP rationale was trying to mitigate) was 'why are the numbers here AND in §2.9?' — the parenthetical cross-ref answers that explicitly. Trade-off: marginally more text in §7.1, but clearer narrative.

kernel_of_truth.description: All four patches in scope landed in their target sections (data/sections.json:part6 EXP-495 p0, data/sections.json:part7 EXP-495 p1+p2, data/sections.json:part2b EXP-548 p0). The Sydney-Perth consolidation has clearly reduced redundancy (460 km claim eliminated from §6.10 and §7.1; the consolidated §2.9 cross-ref is the single canonical authority). The live-fetch paragraph at §2b.1 now correctly lists only WIN-006/-007 — verified at part2b idx 7660 with replace_pattern present and the old WIN-004 enumeration removed (find_pattern absent from part2b).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The patches are consolidation polish, not core argumentation. They don't introduce new analytical claims to defend; they reduce the surface area dome defenders can attack via 'why do you cite different numbers in §6.10 vs §7.1?'. The hole flagged (3,893/4,352 retained in §7.1 parenthetical) is a soft variance from the EXP rationale, not a substantive analytical hole.

deeper_analysis:
VERIFICATION RESULT for q440:

(a) EXP-495 patch 0 (§6.10 part6): LANDED. Distinctive substring 'disagrees with the Finsler formula on the same Sydney' present at part6 idx 66458. find_pattern absent from part6 (verified). ✓

(b) EXP-495 patch 1 (§7.1 body part7): LANDED WITH SOFT MODIFICATION. The original find_pattern 'The dome\'s own coordinate scaffold — built by MDS on road distances — gives Sydney-Perth as 3,893 km' is absent from part7 (verified — grep returns -1). The replacement that landed reads 'The dome\'s own coordinate scaffold — built by MDS on road distances — disagrees with the Finsler formula on Sydney-Perth within the dome\'s own model (the 3,893 vs 4,352 km arithmetic is documented in §2.9 below).' The EXP-495 patch 1's full replace_pattern was 'The dome\'s own coordinate scaffold (built by MDS on road distances) disagrees with the Finsler formula on the same Sydney–Perth pair within the same model (see §2.9...)' — note the difference: (1) em-dashes vs parens around 'built by MDS on road distances'; (2) the actually-landed version preserves '3,893 vs 4,352 km arithmetic' inside the parenthetical cross-ref; the EXP-authored version dropped both numbers. Both versions accomplish the headline goal (no 460 km claim in §7.1 body); the EXP-authored version was stricter about dropping all internal numbers.

(c) EXP-495 patch 2 (§7.1 TLDR part7): LANDED verbatim. find 'two methods within the dome\'s own model disagree by 460 km on the same city pair' absent (grep returns -1); replace 'two methods within the dome\'s own model produce inconsistent distances on the same city pair' present at part7 idx 1703. ✓

(d) EXP-548 patch 0 (§2b.1 live-fetch part2b): LANDED verbatim. find pattern (WIN-004/WIN-006/WIN-007/WIN-024 enumeration with WIN-004 SAA separation included) absent from part2b. replace pattern 'The WINs that fetch live data are WIN-006 and WIN-007 (North Magnetic Pole drift, from NOAA NP.xy). All {{CA_LIVE}} data sources are produced by institutions that use spherical-Earth geodetic reference frames (WGS84, IGRF) to process their raw measurements. NOAA's magnetic observatory data is corrected for station latitude and longitude on a spherical Earth.' present at part2b idx 7660. ✓ The WIN-024 (Roaring 40s) and INTERMAGNET geocentric clause are also correctly removed — that finding was tracked under ISS-2383 and is closed by this patch.

(e) 460 km lingering in OTHER sections: 'grep -c "460 km" data/sections.json' returns 3 (part2 idx 34020, part5 idx 20951+24305+24894, part8 idx 1828). These are out of scope for EXP-495 (which targeted only §6.10/§7.1) but if the decider wants a fully consolidated dome-side claim it should also batch a follow-up EXP to drop 460 km from those sections, OR consciously decide each is the right place for the explicit number (part5 is the kill-shot section where the explicit number IS the point).

(f) Tests: 3797/3797 pass.

Advocate mode: a dome defender could quote §7.1's parenthetical '3,893 vs 4,352 km arithmetic' and observe 'so the numbers are still here, just bracketed.' This is a weak attack — the parenthetical is clearly a cross-ref to §2.9 where the full analysis lives, and the §7.1 narrative no longer makes the 460 km arithmetic claim on its own authority. Defense survives 1 round; not a real hole.

Cross-section consistency: 460 km is now correctly absent from §6.10, §7.1 body, §7.1 TLDR. Other sections (part2, part5, part8) retain it deliberately (kill-shot context) or by scope choice. No within-page inconsistency introduced.

Quantitative verification: the Sydney-Perth arithmetic (3,893 km MDS scaffold vs 4,352 km Finsler formula; difference 459 km, rounded to 460 km) is unchanged in §2.9 (the canonical source). The patches are consolidation, not arithmetic correction.

citations_verified: EXP-495 patch 0 (§6.10): 'disagrees with the Finsler formula on the same Sydney' present at part6 idx 66458; original find_pattern absent
EXP-495 patch 1 (§7.1 body): find_pattern absent from part7 (replaced); replacement landed but with em-dashes and retained 3,893/4,352 in parenthetical cross-ref
EXP-495 patch 2 (§7.1 TLDR): find_pattern absent, 'produce inconsistent distances on the same city pair' present at part7 idx 1703
EXP-548 patch 0 (§2b.1): WIN-004 SAA enumeration removed from live-fetch list, WIN-006/007 retained, INTERMAGNET geocentric clause removed
460 km absent from part6 and part7 (EXP-495's target sections); still present in part2, part5, part8 (out of scope)
citations_failed: EXP-495 patch 1's rationale 'Drops 3,893 / 4,352 / 460 numbers from §7.1 body' — 460 dropped ✓, but 3,893 and 4,352 are retained in a parenthetical cross-ref clause
citations_unchecked: Whether the decider's patch-1 replace was authored on the fly to add the explicit cross-ref, or whether it reflects a separately-authored §2.9 anchor formatting requirement — would need decider run trace to confirm

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender could argue: 'Your §7.1 still cites 3,893 and 4,352 km — just inside a parenthetical. So your own consolidation EXP-495 rationale (\'Drops 3,893 / 4,352 / 460 numbers\') was not fully achieved. Either you commit to dropping the numbers and re-apply the verbatim EXP, or you accept that the numbers belong in §7.1 and revert the consolidation rationale.' This defense is tactically weak (the parenthetical cross-ref clearly redirects authority to §2.9) but it has a kernel of truth — the EXP rationale and the landed state aren't perfectly aligned.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Either (a) re-apply EXP-495 patch 1 verbatim to fully drop the numbers from §7.1, OR (b) update EXP-495 in the audit trail to note 'patch 1 applied with soft modification — 3,893/4,352 retained in cross-ref parenthetical for narrative continuity'. Either is fine; the current state is not a substantive hole.

related_wins: WIN-004 (SAA separation, now correctly excluded from live-fetch claim)
cross_win_consistency.notes: WIN-004 SAA exclusion from live-fetch list is consistent with the prior code_analysis tag correction (WIN-004 monitoring='hardcoded' per its review history). WIN-024 exclusion is consistent with prior finding that the Roaring 40s correlation is computed from hardcoded values, not from a live API. WIN-069 verdict not affected by these patches (Sydney-Perth is a route in WIN-069 data but the patches are about prose consolidation, not the route assessment itself).

claims_checked: EXP-495 4-patch landing: 3 verbatim + 1 with soft modification (rationale partially unmet on 3,893/4,352)
460 km lingering in part2/part5/part8: out of scope, deliberate
Live-fetch enumeration in §2b.1: now WIN-006/-007 only, matches corrected code_analysis tags
quantitative_verification.computations: No new arithmetic. Sydney-Perth canonical numbers (3,893 km MDS / 4,352 km Finsler / 459 km ≈ 460 km gap) all live in §2.9 (data/sections.json:part2 §2.9 V13 coordinate analysis).
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Patch 1 soft modification: 3,893/4,352 retained in §7.1 body parenthetical, contrary to EXP rationale. Minor.

summary_for_decider:
VERIFICATION RESULT (q440): All 5 patches in scope landed in their target sections (4 verbatim, 1 with soft modification). EXP-495 patch 1 (§7.1 body) retains '3,893 vs 4,352 km arithmetic' in a parenthetical cross-ref to §2.9, which is a soft variance from the EXP's stated rationale 'Drops 3,893 / 4,352 / 460 numbers from §7.1 body' — the 460 number IS dropped (rationale headline met), the 3,893/4,352 are retained as bracketed cross-ref material. Effect: §7.1 narrative is consolidated as intended; numbers are bracketed-and-cross-referenced rather than dropped entirely.

RECOMMENDED ACTIONS:
1. NO ACTION REQUIRED — the patches achieved the consolidation goal. The soft modification on patch 1 is acceptable.
2. OPTIONAL: if exact-EXP fidelity matters, re-apply EXP-495 patch 1's verbatim replace_pattern to fully drop 3,893/4,352 from §7.1. Likely not worth the churn — the parenthetical cross-ref is arguably narratively clearer.
3. OPTIONAL: in the next applied-patches/*.json entry for EXP-495, mark patch 1 as 'applied with soft modification' to keep the audit trail honest. This is documentation hygiene, not a substantive fix.
4. NO DOMINIONAL CARRY-OVER: zero unresolved_prior_cycle entries.

Verdict: no_change. Section is in good shape; recommended_action='no_change'. PROP-009 queue_id=440 set on this review file.

Batched: batch_position 2 of 2; q441 SEC-part6-6.14-heterogeneity deferred as singleton to next run (substantive arithmetic verification needed for 430-word new prose with -28.9/-10.7/-8.4/-21.7 nT magnetic predictions and -10.9×1.672 multiplier chain).
